authoritative_sources.title: 43-4081.00 - Hotel, Motel, and Resort Desk Clerks
authoritative_sources.publisher: O*NET OnLine, sponsored by the U.S. Department of Labor
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: O*NET is a U.S. Department of Labor-sponsored occupational data source, and this occupation directly describes front-desk tasks including guest registration, room assignment, key issuance, reservation confirmation, credit verification, room availability records, guest account records, PMS software, and referral to managers.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: arrival intake, registration, room assignment readiness, key-control prerequisite review, reservation confirmation, and payment-guarantee validation
used_for: PMS, room, payment, and account-record expectations

authoritative_sources.title: Americans with Disabilities Act Title III Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOJ is the primary federal civil-rights authority for ADA Title III enforcement, and the lodging reservations section directly governs accessible-room descriptions, reservation-system handling, and room blocking controls that affect front-office arrival readiness.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: accessible-room reservation verification, feature-description dependency handling, and accessible-room readiness escalation

authoritative_sources.title: The Rule on Unfair or Deceptive Fees: Frequently Asked Questions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection regulator, and this official short-term-lodging guidance defines mandatory-fee and total-price disclosure constraints for hotels, vacation rentals, platforms, and travel agents.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: rate-plan, resort-fee, amenity-fee, and booking-disclosure mismatch handling before check-in
used_for: front-office pricing and fee override refusal
conflict handling between public baseline and tenant fee scripts

authoritative_sources.title: Refunds
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOT is the primary U.S. aviation consumer-protection regulator, and this official refund guidance defines cancellation, significant-change, ancillary-service, ticket-agent, merchant-of-record, and 24-hour cancellation constraints for travel itineraries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: itinerary readiness, ticket-agent or merchant-of-record routing, travel refund boundary handling, and supplier escalation
used_for: travel itinerary refund and rebooking boundary
front-office refusal to adjudicate airline or supplier refund entitlement

authoritative_sources.title: How to fix mistakes in your credit card bill
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CFPB is the primary U.S. consumer-finance regulator for billing-error guidance and is authoritative for folio dispute, duplicate charge, refund-not-posted, and issuer-versus-merchant boundary handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment guarantee, folio mismatch, duplicate charge, and refund-not-posted intake routing

authoritative_sources.title: Fighting Identity Theft with the Red Flags Rule: A How-To Guide for Business
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC Red Flags guidance is an authoritative public control reference for suspicious identity, altered identification, covered-account access, and account-change patterns that affect front-office identity checks, remote check-in, profile updates, and payment reroute requests.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: identity mismatch, profile-change, payment-reroute, remote check-in, and fraud-sensitive arrival handling
used_for: remote check-in and account-change refusal triggers

authoritative_sources.title: Travel-associated Outbreaks as a Public Health Issue
authoritative_sources.publisher: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CDC is the primary U.S. public-health authority, and this accommodation-specific outbreak guidance directly addresses guest-list requests, notifying incoming or past guests, and escalation behavior after suspected exposure events at accommodations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: safety-sensitive arrival intake, guest-list preservation, incoming-guest notification boundary, and public-health escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Are call center environments considered sensitive areas for PCI DSS Requirement 9.1.1?
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCI SSC is the recognized payment-card security standards body, and this guidance constrains workflows that process or transmit cardholder data during payment guarantee, preauthorization, and front-desk or contact-center card handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: payment preauthorization, card-data collection boundary, and payment-security escalation

workflow_stages.name: Arrival request intake and source grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm property or station, guest or traveler identity context, confirmation or itinerary reference, arrival date, channel, and whether the request is in lane before any check-in or routing decision.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Reservation, itinerary, profile, and payment prerequisite review
workflow_stages.description: Validate PMS, booking engine, CRM, payment guarantee, folio, voucher, and itinerary records so arrival readiness is tied to authoritative systems rather than a guest assertion or incomplete channel note.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Room, accessibility, and itinerary readiness check
workflow_stages.description: Confirm room status, housekeeping inspection, accessible-room constraints, out-of-order notes, late-arrival notes, supplier itinerary readiness, and key-control prerequisites before check-in or service execution begins.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Eligibility and authorization disposition
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether the guest or traveler is ready, blocked, rejected, missing facts, or owner-routed based on reservation, payment, identity, room or itinerary readiness, and tenant policy.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Pre-execution routing and escalation
workflow_stages.description: Route booking mismatch, payment guarantee, room readiness, itinerary, accessibility, refund, pricing, VIP, safety, or fraud blockers to the correct owner with evidence before downstream execution begins.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: High-volume arrival queue control
workflow_stages.description: Segment arrivals and service-speed pressure by ready, blocked, missing-fact, exception-sensitive, safety or fraud stop, and adjacent-owner queues while preserving prerequisite validation.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

artifacts: reservation confirmations, confirmation numbers, booking-source references, rate-plan terms, cancellation terms, and late-arrival notes
PMS profiles, guest records, folio snapshots, incidental holds, payment guarantees, preauthorization records, vouchers, and merchant-of-record references
room-status board, housekeeping clean or inspected status, out-of-order logs, room move restrictions, key-control prerequisites, and walk list notes
accessible-room feature notes, accessibility reservation records, alternate-format or accommodation notes, and inventory hold instructions
itinerary segments, supplier confirmations, rebooking records, ancillary-service records, and travel-agent or OTA references
CRM notes, prior contacts, guest-service histories, escalation records, VIP flags, and service-recovery approval histories
fraud or loss-prevention flags, identity mismatch notes, safety or public-health notices, incident records, and payment-security handling logs
systems_of_record: room-status, housekeeping, or front-desk operations system

allowed: Classify arrival intake as ready, blocked, rejected, missing facts, exception-sensitive, or owned by another lane.
Determine whether records support check-in eligibility, identity verification, payment guarantee, room readiness, itinerary readiness, or routing before execution begins.
Identify the blocker as room readiness, booking mismatch, payment guarantee, identity issue, accessible-room dependency, itinerary dependency, safety or fraud stop, or tenant-policy authority.
Prepare front-office disposition notes, prerequisite checklists, routing notes, and arrival escalation summaries tied to authoritative records.
escalate: Reservation, folio, payment, identity, room-status, accessible-room, or itinerary records are missing, stale, or materially conflict.
The request requires room-control, walk decision, pricing override, refund exception, fee waiver, compensation approval, legal interpretation, fraud adjudication, or safety signoff.
Customer harm, public-health, suspicious identity, loss-prevention, or payment-data-handling signals change whether check-in or routing can proceed.
Public baseline guidance and tenant, brand, supplier, processor, property, or jurisdiction policy materially conflict.
refuse: Clear check-in, key issuance, remote check-in, or itinerary routing when prerequisite records do not support readiness.
Override pricing, refund, room readiness, accessible-room, VIP, fraud, safety, or payment-security policies without explicit authority.
Ask for or restate full payment-card data in insecure front-desk, phone, email, chat, or SMS workflows.
Make legal, fraud, safety, or public-health determinations outside documented intake authority.

domain_failure_modes: the lane clears check-in despite mismatched reservation dates, absent payment guarantee, unresolved identity signals, or room not inspected
a booking, folio, itinerary, voucher, or merchant-of-record mismatch is handled as generic customer service instead of record-linked front-office routing
front-office pressure from VIP status, queue length, or guest frustration becomes an unsupported room, rate, refund, or fee exception
accessible-room reservation or room-feature constraints are bypassed without actual inventory and policy verification
payment preauthorization or card handling proceeds through insecure channels or incomplete PCI-aware controls
safety, public-health, trafficking, security, or fraud indicators are treated as routine arrival blockers instead of stop and escalate cases
the industry overlay collapses into generic intake language instead of hospitality reservation, folio, room-status, itinerary, payment, and key-control terminology

scenario_family_coverage.coverage_note: Scenarios cover arrival versus reservation date conflict, modified PMS stay date, stale payment preauthorization, OTA voucher, merchant-of-record, folio, and incidental-hold terminology.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
scenario_family_coverage.coverage_note: Scenarios cover VIP pressure, fee waiver and refund demands, room-readiness conflict, accessible-room policy conflict, and escalation rather than override.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications
scenario_family_coverage.coverage_note: Scenarios cover public-health exposure notice handling, guest-list artifacts, remote check-in, profile change, alternate card use, identity mismatch, and fraud routing.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Jurisdiction-specific lodging registration and ID rules
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The public baseline does not create a universal front-desk ID rule. The pack requires tenant and jurisdiction retrieval for ID, age, residency, tax, and registration requirements before check-in disposition.
The pack treats clean, inspected, out-of-order, do-not-move, and early-arrival rules as tenant-controlled prerequisites. Conflicts with public accessibility or safety baselines require escalation.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Supplier and travel-agent authority for itinerary changes
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: The pack routes supplier, airline, OTA, and merchant-of-record cases using retrieved tenant and supplier rules and refuses to adjudicate refund entitlement or rebooking authority in-lane.
